Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |\n| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |\n| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n## Quick PK Readout\n- No obvious anomalies in the current patient-level dosing parameters.\n- CU-001 and CU-002 appear clinically stable based on notes.\n- CU-003 is newly enrolled, so trend stability is still limited by shorter follow-up.\n- A full PK model stability assessment would require current validation/log outputs, not just the dosing config.\n",

)]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and prepared a summary for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`:

- **CU-001**: 12.4 kg, **0.15 mg/kg** → **1.86 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-14**
- **CU-002**: 9.1 kg, **0.12 mg/kg** → **1.09 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-12**
- **CU-003**: 15.7 kg, **0.18 mg/kg** → **2.83 mg**, **q2w**, next dose **2026-04-10**

PK-related values in the config:
- **CU-001:** 3.2 mL/min
- **CU-002:** 2.8 mL/min
- **CU-003:** 4.1 mL/min

Based on the config alone, things look **generally stable**:
- **CU-001**: noted as stable with 22% tumor regression
- **CU-002**: responding well, weight gain improving
- **CU-003**: newly enrolled, so less longitudinal stability data so far

One caveat: this is a **dosing/config review**, not a full PK model health check. I don’t see any obvious anomalies in the current parameters, but confirming full model stability would require the latest validation or runtime logs.

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter email-ready note once you give me Dr. Martinez’s email.
